popularStory.headline: St. Margaret of Scotland
popularStory.description: St. Margaret of Scotland, or Margaret of Wessex, was an English princess born in Hungary to Princess Agatha of Hungary and English Prince Edward the Exile around 1045. Her siblings, Cristina and Edgar …

zh.lifeSummary: 當St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotland於1045年9月8日出生在匈牙利巴拉亞時，她的父親Edward Ætheling the Exile29歲，母親Agatha Von Brunswick Princess Of Hungary, Of Kiev Yaroslavna27歲。她於1068年與Malcolm III King of Scotland在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1093年11月16日在蘇格蘭艾丁波享年48歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭費伏登福姆林。

zh-hans.Dunfermline, Fife, Scotland: 英国苏格兰費伏登福姆林
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotland于1045年9月8日出生在匈牙利巴拉亞时，她的父亲Edward Ætheling the Exile29岁，母亲Agatha Von Brunswick Princess Of Hungary, Of Kiev Yaroslavna27岁。她于1068年与Malcolm III King of Scotland在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1093年11月16日在苏格兰艾丁波享年48岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰費伏登福姆林。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Wessex,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Messick   1, a surname of unclear French origin.
History: Bearers of the surname Messex are descendants of Julian Messick from France (see Messick ).

variants.name: Messick
meanings: Americanized form of an unidentified French surname, presumably Messac: habitational name from any of several places with this name (e.g. in Ille-et-Vilaine, Brittany), named in Gallo-Roman times as the estate of someone called Mettius. Compare Messex and Mezick .
In some cases an Americanized form of Croatian (and possibly also Bosniak) Mesić and Mešić, Slovenian Mešič (see Mesic , compare Mesick 2).
In some cases an Americanized form of Slovak Mesík: unexplained. Compare Mesick 3.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

en.lifeSummary: When St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotland was born on 8 September 1045, in Baranya, Hungary, her father, Edward Ætheling the Exile, was 29 and her mother, Agatha Von Brunswick Princess Of Hungary, Of Kiev Yaroslavna, was 27. She married Malcolm III King of Scotland in 1068, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 16 November 1093, in Edinburgh, Scotland, at the age of 48, and was buried in Dunfermline, Fife, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotland est née le 8 septembre 1045, à Baranya, Hongrie, son père, Edward Ætheling the Exile, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Agatha Von Brunswick Princess Of Hungary, Of Kiev Yaroslavna, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé Malcolm III King of Scotland en 1068, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 16 novembre 1093, à Edinburgh, Écosse, à l’âge de 48 ans, et a été enterrée à Dunfermline, Fife, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotland am 8. September 1045 in Baranya, Ungarn geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Edward Ætheling the Exile 29 und ihre Mutter Agatha Von Brunswick Princess Of Hungary, Of Kiev Yaroslavna 27. Sie hat 1068 in Scotland Malcolm III King of Scotland geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 16. November 1093 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Edinburgh, Scotland und wurde in Dunfermline, Fife, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotland è nata il il 8 settembre 1045 nel Baranya, Ungheria, sua padre, Edward Ætheling the Exile, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Agatha Von Brunswick Princess Of Hungary, Of Kiev Yaroslavna, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato Malcolm III King of Scotland nel 1068, nel Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 16 novembre 1093, nel Edinburgh, Scotland, all'età di 48 anni, e fu sepolta a Dunfermline, Fife, Scozia, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotlandが1045年9月8日にハンガリーバーアニーアで生まれた時，父Edward Ætheling the Exileは29歳，母Agatha Von Brunswick Princess Of Hungary, Of Kiev Yaroslavnaは27歳であった。1068年にスコットランドでMalcolm III King of Scotlandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1093年11月16日にスコットランドエディンブルグで48歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドフィフダンフエルムラインにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotland(이)가 1045년 9월 8일 헝가리 버랜야에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edward Ætheling the Exile(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agatha Von Brunswick Princess Of Hungary, Of Kiev Yaroslavna(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1068년 스컷랜드에서 Malcolm III King of Scotland(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1093년 11월 16일 스컷랜드 에딘버그에서 48세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 피프 던퍼라이인에 묻혔다.

pt.8 September 1045–16 November 1093: 8 de setembro de 1045–16 de novembro de 1093
pt.lifeSummary: Quando St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotland nasceu em 8 de setembro de 1045, em Baranya, Hungria, seu pai, Edward Ætheling the Exile, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Agatha Von Brunswick Princess Of Hungary, Of Kiev Yaroslavna, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com Malcolm III King of Scotland em 1068, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 16 de novembro de 1093, em Edinburgh, Scotland, com 48 anos, e foi sepultada em Dunfermline, Fife, Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotland родилась 8 сентября 1045 г. в местности бараня, Венгрия, её отцу по имени Edward Ætheling the Exile было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Agatha Von Brunswick Princess Of Hungary, Of Kiev Yaroslavna, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Malcolm III King of Scotland в 1068 г. в местности Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла 16 ноября 1093 г. в местности эдинбург, Шотландия в возрасте 48 лет и была похоронена в местности дунфирмлаин, фиф, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.8 September 1045–16 November 1093: 8 de septiembre de 1045–16 de noviembre de 1093
es.lifeSummary: Cuando St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotland nació el 8 de septiembre de 1045, en Baranya, Hungría, su papá, Edward Ætheling the Exile, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Agatha Von Brunswick Princess Of Hungary, Of Kiev Yaroslavna, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Malcolm III King of Scotland en 1068, en Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 16 de noviembre de 1093, en Edinburgh, Scotland, a la edad de 48 años, y fue sepultada en Dunfermline, Fife, Escocia, Reino Unido.
